_comment: AUDIT TEST CASE: Combined multiple REF + multiple ENCL entries - tests ordering, numbering format, and spacing between sections
subj: AUDIT TEST FOR COMBINED MULTIPLE REFERENCE AND ENCLOSURE ENTRIES WITH LONG TEXT TO VERIFY ORDERING AND SPACING
ref: (a) SECNAV M-5216.5 Ch-2 with extensive supplementary material covering records management procedures
(b) MCO 5216.11 which provides detailed guidance on correspondence preparation and formatting standards
(c) NAVADMIN 2024/156 announcing updates to the records management system implementation timeline
(d) OPNAVINST 5215.17A establishing the Department of the Navy directives and records management program
encl: (1) Records Management Procedures Manual with comprehensive guidance on filing and retrieval operations
(2) Correspondence Preparation Handbook covering formatting standards for all types of military letters
(3) Implementation Timeline Chart showing key milestones for the new records management system deployment
(4) Training Schedule and Attendance Roster for upcoming records management certification courses
body: 1. This is the first paragraph of the audit test case for combined REF and ENCL entries. The purpose of this document is to verify that the renderer correctly handles both reference and enclosure sections with proper ordering and spacing.
2. This is the second paragraph. The audit will verify that REF entries appear before ENCL entries in the header block, as required by SECNAV correspondence standards.

3. This is the third paragraph. The spacing from the last REF entry to the first ENCL entry should be exactly one leading unit (14.4 points), and the spacing from the last ENCL entry to the first body paragraph should also be exactly one leading unit.

4. This is the fourth paragraph. Both REF and ENCL entries contain intentionally long text to test whether continuation lines return to the left margin rather than being indented under the marker text.
5. This is the fifth paragraph. The audit will verify that REF uses lowercase letter numbering (a), (b), (c), (d) while ENCL uses Arabic numeral numbering (1), (2), (3), (4).
6. This is the sixth and final paragraph. This combined test case ensures that spacing does not stack or accumulate incorrectly when both REF and ENCL sections are present with multiple entries each.
